Now I will teach you Same.<[silence:60ms]>

Look at two rocks—one in left hand, one in right.<[silence:60ms]> If both heavy like bear, both smooth like river stone, both gray like storm sky—then same.<[silence:60ms]> Not if one sharp, one round.<[silence:60ms]> Same means no difference when you touch, see, or taste.<[silence:60ms]> Like two berries from same bush, two sticks from same tree.<[silence:60ms]> If swap, nothing changes.<[silence:60ms]>
clean_story:
Now I will teach you Same.

Look at two rocks—one in left hand, one in right. If both heavy like bear, both smooth like river stone, both gray like storm sky—then same. Not if one sharp, one round. Same means no difference when you touch, see, or taste. Like two berries from same bush, two sticks from same tree. If swap, nothing changes.
